title: Her Eyes Are Bluebells Now
content:
Her eyes are bluebells now, her voice a bird,
        And the long sighing grass her elegy;
    She who a woman was is now a star
        In the high heaven shining down on me.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Le Gallienne delivers a powerful performance in "Her Eyes Are Bluebells Now"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
